intro: The Department of Investigation was founded in 1873 as the Office of the Commissioner of Accounts, created in response to the looting of City funds by Boss Tweed, making it one of the oldest law-enforcement agencies in the country. As the City's independent inspector general, DOI investigates and refers for prosecution cases of fraud and corruption by City employees, contractors, and others doing business with the City; it also vets the backgrounds of City workers earning over $125,000 and companies awarded contracts of $250,000 or more, and, since 2014, has run the NYPD's own Office of the Inspector General.

cardHighlights.function: Investigates fraud and corruption by City employees and contractors
cardHighlights.scale: Conducted more than 1,700 background investigations in FY2024
